intent: recipe app for people who hate cooking

body.intent: recipe app for people who hate cooking

draft.title: recipe app for people who hate cooking
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
recipe app for people who hate cooking

Building your first draft…

data.intent: recipe app for people who hate cooking

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking prefer warm, encouraging language over efficiency-speak
facade.label: Lazy Sunday tone
facade.acceptImplies: Voice should feel like a friend helping you out, not a drill sergeant. Casual, reassuring, maybe a little humorous about the cooking struggle.
facade.rejectImplies: Users want crisp, no-nonsense, get-it-done language. Skip the warmth—just tell me what to do.

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking prefer gentle, approachable interfaces over sharp, intimidating design
facade.label: Soft rounded corners
facade.acceptImplies: Design should feel inviting and low-pressure, with curved buttons and smooth edges throughout the app
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer crisp, modern, or no-nonsense design—sharp corners and clean lines feel more appropriate for a practical tool

evidence.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking prefer warm, encouraging language over efficiency-speak
evidence.implication: Voice should feel like a friend helping you out, not a drill sergeant. Casual, reassuring, maybe a little humorous about the cooking struggle.

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking respond better to approachable, human-made aesthetic than sterile efficiency
facade.label: Warm, handwritten
facade.acceptImplies: App should feel like a friend's messy recipe note, not a database. Hand-drawn elements, imperfect typography, analog warmth.
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers clean, modern, digital-first interface. Likely wants efficiency signals and polish over coziness.

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer smooth, animated ingredient reveals over instant static lists — reduces cognitive load and creates delight in a low-effort cooking context
facade.label: Ingredients slide in
facade.acceptImplies: Motion-forward design: expect user preference for fluid micro-interactions, loading states that feel alive, and gestural navigation over clicks
facade.rejectImplies: Motion-skeptical design: user may prefer direct, snappy interfaces that prioritize speed and clarity over animation — fewer transitions, more instant feedback

facade.hypothesis: Users want recipes shown by cooking duration, not ingredients or steps
facade.axisTargeted: Dense data vs. key metrics — should recipe selection prioritize time-to-eat as the dominant metric?
facade.label: Time in minutes
facade.acceptImplies: Lead with duration. Show "15 min" before ingredients. Filter/sort by time first. Recipe cards display cook time prominently.
facade.rejectImplies: Time is secondary. Lead with what you're making or ingredients available. Bury duration in small text. Users browse by appetite, not clock.

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking prefer compact, always-visible navigation over tab switching—reduces cognitive load when hungry and impatient.
facade.label: Sidebar nav
facade.acceptImplies: Lean toward persistent left/right sidebars for recipe app; users want quick access to filters, saved recipes, and shopping list without mode-switching.
facade.rejectImplies: Lean toward tab bars or collapsible navigation; users tolerate or prefer tab-based navigation, suggesting tabs might feel less cluttered or more mobile-friendly.

axes.label: Whether user wants visual simplicity and minimal ingredient lists vs. rich food photography and detailed plating inspiration
Whether user prefers step-by-step guided workflows vs. ingredient-first browsing and flexible navigation
Whether user responds to encouraging, motivational tone vs. irreverent, humorous tone that validates cooking aversion
Whether user wants calm, meditative cooking experience vs. quick-win, dopamine-hit satisfaction from easy wins
Whether user needs heavy filtering (dietary, time, skill) upfront vs. minimal friction and discovery-based browsing
axes.poleA: Lots of filters or keep it simple

axes.label: Whether user wants instant gratification feedback (timers, notifications) vs. passive, low-pressure experience with minimal alerts
axes.poleA: Alerts and timers or quiet mode

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants visual simplicity and minimal ingredient lists vs. rich food photography and detailed plating inspiration
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers step-by-step guided workflows vs. ingredient-first browsing and flexible navigation
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user needs heavy filtering (dietary, time, skill) upfront vs. minimal friction and discovery-based browsing
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant gratification feedback (timers, notifications) vs. passive, low-pressure experience with minimal alerts
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking respond better to commanding, energetic language than soft reassurance
facade.axisTargeted: Voice and Personality — tone intensity (encouraging vs commanding)
facade.label: All-caps headlines
facade.acceptImplies: Voice should feel bold and motivating; users want to feel pumped up about cooking despite resistance
facade.rejectImplies: Voice should remain gentle and understated; all-caps feels aggressive and reinforces cooking anxiety

draft.title: QuickBite – Recipe App for People Who Hate Cooking
draft.summary: A recipe app that strips away complexity and delivers only the fastest, easiest meals with minimal ingredients and steps. Perfect for busy people who want food without the fuss.
draft.html:
QuickBite
Meals in 15 minutes or less. No excuses.
Filter by Time
Today's Easiest Meals
Pasta Aglio e Olio
⏱ 8 min
🛒 4 ingredients
Scrambled Eggs & Toast
⏱ 5 min
🛒 3 ingredients
Tuna Salad Wrap
⏱ 6 min
🛒 5 ingredients
